params.name: Theory of the Four Elements
params.description: The Theory of the Four Elements is a theory about the composition of matter proposed by ancient Greek philosophers. The theory posits that all matter in the world is composed of four basic elements: earth, water, air, and fire. The proponent ofThis theory, Empedocles, believed that these four elements combine and separate under the influence of two forces, love and strife, forming all things. This theory was later developed and refined by Aristotle, who introduced a fifth element, aether, to exp …
